title: Decision Queue
summary: Your prioritised action list for the day, ordered by urgency, deadline, and value at stake.
why_it_matters: Use this screen first when you need to understand what must be handled now and which trading decisions carry the biggest immediate impact.
steps: Start with the highest-priority card and review the deadline and impact summary.
Open the reasoning view if you need more context before acting.
Use the primary action, snooze, or AI briefing tools to move the queue forward.
tasks: Open a specific decision and review the reasoning.
Complete or snooze a decision item.
quick_questions: What is the most urgent decision right now?
chat_examples: What is the most urgent decision right now?

sections.title: What you see
items: A live greeting bar and key decision KPIs.
Decision cards grouped by urgency with deadlines and impact.
An AI briefing area for a market-aware summary.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Summarising the queue into a trader briefing.
Explaining why a decision was prioritised.
Opening the next screen for the selected decision.

automation_examples: Snooze the selected decision for 30 minutes.

title: Trader Dashboard
summary: A live desk overview of performance, alerts, news, blotter activity, and risk hotspots.
why_it_matters: This is the fastest way to understand current desk state before you drill into positions, intelligence, or market structure.
steps: Scan the headline KPI strip to understand P&L, VaR, decisions, and alerts.
Use the tile layout to inspect the cards showing the biggest active issues.
Drill into Positions, AI Intelligence, or Market Data when a tile needs deeper investigation.
tasks: Inspect alerts, blotter activity, or heat map hotspots.
quick_questions: Show me my top risk from the dashboard.
chat_examples: Show me my top risk from the dashboard.

sections.title: What you see
items: Desk KPIs at the top of the page.
Tiles for P&L, alerts, heat maps, news, and blotter activity.
Layout controls so users can tune the workspace.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Explaining unusual P&L moves.
Summarising the most important alerts and news.
Pointing you to the next page to investigate.

title: Positions & Risk
summary: Detailed position, hedge, exposure, and VaR analysis across the desk.
why_it_matters: Use this page when you need to understand where risk sits, how well the book is hedged, and which exposures deserve action.
steps: Filter between physical and financial exposures to narrow the book.
Use the position grid to identify large exposures, weak hedge ratios, and major VaR contributors.
Move to Market Data or AI Intelligence when you need scenario or hedge analysis.
features: Position grid with exposure and P&L
tasks: Review hedge ratios and commodity VaR.
quick_questions: Take me to the Brent curve.
chat_examples: Take me to the Brent curve.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Highlighting the biggest exposure on the screen.
Explaining risk concentration in plain English.
Sending you to the next page for action.

title: AI Intelligence Centre
summary: The desk's AI workspace for hedge advice, trade ideas, anomalies, forecasts, and pre-mortems.
why_it_matters: This screen concentrates the platform's highest-value AI workflows, so it is the fastest place to move from raw risk to actionable recommendations.
steps: Choose the relevant commodity or book area before running an analysis.
Use Hedge Advisor when exposure is known and you want a response structure.
Use Pre-mortem or Forecast tools when you need scenario-oriented guidance.
tasks: Run Hedge Advisor for a selected commodity.
Run the pre-mortem on the current book.
quick_questions: Run a pre-mortem on my book.
Generate a forecast narrative for Brent.
chat_examples: Run a pre-mortem on my book.
Generate a forecast narrative for Brent.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Running supported modules directly from chat.
Explaining output in trader-friendly language.
Connecting AI findings to the right execution screen.

automation_examples: Run a pre-mortem on my book.
Generate a forecast narrative for Brent.

title: Performance & Analytics
summary: Performance tracking against plan, including run rate, attribution, and missed-opportunity context.
why_it_matters: Use this page when you need to explain whether the desk is on track and what is driving outperformance or shortfall.
steps: Start with target attainment and forecast metrics to judge trajectory.
Use attribution views to understand what drove gains or shortfalls.
Move into Decision Intelligence when you need forensic explanation.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Summarising why the desk is ahead or behind plan.
Explaining the biggest contributors to performance.
Guiding you toward missed-opportunity analysis.

title: Decision Intelligence
summary: Forensic review of missed decisions and institutional memory search for similar situations.
why_it_matters: This is where the desk can understand not only what happened, but why value was left on the table and which historical patterns matter now.
steps: Run forensics when you want a structured post-mortem of desk behaviour.
Use Desk Brain when you need precedent or pattern recall.
Cross-reference with Performance or Boardroom when telling the wider story.
tasks: Search Desk Brain for similar trades.
quick_questions: What did we miss last quarter?
Search Desk Brain for Urals arb history.
chat_examples: What did we miss last quarter?
Search Desk Brain for Urals arb history.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Launching forensics from chat.
Searching historical structures through Desk Brain.
Summarising why an opportunity was missed.

automation_examples: Search Desk Brain for Urals arb history.

title: Market Data & Curves
summary: Live market prices, curve structure, spreads, and scenario testing for key commodities.
why_it_matters: This page connects live market structure to trading decisions, especially when you need to test how price moves would affect the book.
steps: Pick a curve and review the current shape before running scenarios.
Use the scenario box to describe a move in plain language.
Compare the output with Positions & Risk when testing book sensitivity.
quick_questions: Refresh market data and open the Brent curve.
Shift Brent up $3 and show the impact.
chat_examples: Refresh market data and open the Brent curve.
Shift Brent up $3 and show the impact.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Navigating to the correct commodity curve.
Applying supported curve shifts for you.
Explaining current market moves and related headlines.

automation_examples: Refresh market data and open the Brent curve.
Shift Brent up $3 and show the impact.

title: Vessels & Logistics
summary: Operational view of fleet activity, cargo timing, route status, and voyage economics.
why_it_matters: Use this page whenever vessel timing or cargo routing could change exposure, execution timing, or economics.
steps: Check the current delay and route signals for operationally sensitive cargoes.
Open voyage economics when a vessel issue has commercial impact.
Move to Communications or Positions if a delay requires action.
features: Operational context tied to trading impact
tasks: Inspect vessel route and voyage economics.
Review delay impact on trading decisions.
Monitor cargo timing and vessel status.
quick_questions: Will the Innovation delay affect my hedge?
Show me voyage economics for Hermod.
Explain the impact of current vessel delays.
chat_examples: Will the Innovation delay affect my hedge?
Show me voyage economics for Hermod.
Explain the impact of current vessel delays.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Explaining whether a delay matters to the desk.
Summarising voyage economics in plain language.
Guiding you to the next operational or trading step.

title: Communications Hub
summary: Action-oriented inbox with AI summaries, linked trade context, and drafted responses.
why_it_matters: This page turns communications into an operational queue so the desk can respond quickly without losing business context.
steps: Start with critical and high-priority items first.
Open a message to review the AI summary and draft response.
Use mark-actioned after the operational step has been completed.
tasks: Send the drafted reply for the selected email.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Opening a specific email for you.
Sending a prepared reply on supported records.
Summarising the inbox into an action list.

title: Compliance & Audit
summary: Regulatory status, filing awareness, and audit trail visibility for desk operations.
why_it_matters: Use this page for confidence checks before or after sensitive actions and to stay ahead of reporting obligations.
steps: Review the current regulatory posture and upcoming deadlines.
Use the audit trail when you need to confirm who did what and when.
Follow up on deadlines proactively before issues escalate.
quick_questions: What is my next filing deadline?
chat_examples: What is my next filing deadline?

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Summarising current compliance posture.
Pointing you to the next filing or review item.
Explaining how audit information is organised.

title: Help Center
summary: Built-in help workspace that explains every screen, workflow, and supported AI action inside the app.
why_it_matters: This is the fastest way to learn the platform, jump to the right screen, and discover what Radiant AI can do for you.
steps: Use the search bar to find a screen, workflow, prompt, or action.
Choose a topic from the left navigation to read the details on the right.
Use Open Screen, Ask Agent, or Page Help to move from reading to action.
features: Direct links into workflows and AI actions
tasks: Browse grouped help topics without leaving the app.
Open a linked screen from the guide.
Ask Radiant AI to explain or perform a supported task.
quick_questions: How do I use the Decision Queue?
Show me where connectors are configured.
chat_examples: How do I use the Decision Queue?
Show me where connectors are configured.

sections.title: What you see
items: A grouped navigation tree on the left.
Detailed guidance, prompts, and actions on the right.
Search tools that run locally from source-controlled help files.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Answering questions about any screen in the guide.
Navigating you to the page you need.
Performing supported in-app actions directly from chat.

title: AI Studio
summary: The enterprise control plane for AI prompts, agent definitions, tool permissions, and user context.
why_it_matters: Use this page to safely tune how AI behaves across the platform without editing backend code or hidden configuration.
steps: Inspect the default chat agent before changing prompts or permissions.
Use the test harness after every meaningful agent change.
Update the user profile to improve how AI responds across the app.
tasks: Inspect the current chat copilot configuration.
Edit or save an agent definition.
Run a safe test against a selected screen context.
quick_questions: What context does Chat Copilot see?
How should I tune this prompt safely?
chat_examples: What context does Chat Copilot see?
How should I tune this prompt safely?

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Explaining what the current chat agent can access.
Guiding you to the relevant AI Studio control.
Helping you validate whether a prompt change is safe.

title: External Systems Configuration
summary: Control centre for connectors that power feeds, AI providers, and external trading systems.
why_it_matters: This page governs the integrations the rest of the product depends on, so it is the first place to check when data or AI behavior looks wrong.
steps: Open this page whenever feeds or AI providers need setup or validation.
Use test actions after updating credentials or endpoints.
Keep connector naming clear so users and AI can reference them consistently.
quick_questions: Where do I update API keys?
chat_examples: Where do I update API keys?

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Taking you straight to Configuration from chat.
Testing a supported connector for you.
Explaining which connector powers which capability.

title: Boardroom View
summary: Executive summary view of desk performance, strategic upside, and capital efficiency.
why_it_matters: This page reframes trading performance for leadership, helping management understand strategic uplift and cross-book results quickly.
steps: Start with uplift and gap analysis before drilling into source screens.
Use this page when the audience is executive rather than trading.
Move to Performance or Decision Intelligence for supporting detail.
tasks: Compare desk or book performance at a higher level.
quick_questions: What is the top-quartile uplift potential?
chat_examples: What is the top-quartile uplift potential?

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Producing a concise management summary.
Explaining uplift potential and performance gaps.
Pointing you to the supporting operational detail.

title: Admin / Demo Control
summary: Operational control area for scenarios, system health, and administrative shortcuts.
why_it_matters: Use this page for environment checks, demo control, and scenario management that affect what other parts of the app display.
steps: Use this page for environment confidence checks and demo control.
Trigger scenarios carefully because they change what other screens show.
Review service health before debugging missing AI or data behaviour.
tasks: Review AI connectivity and demo controls.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Launching supported scenarios for you.
Guiding you to the right admin control.
Explaining which environment issue to check next.
